title: podman-py login support
body:
use the compat auth endpoint to allow users to access login from within podman-py

resolves #189

Signed-off-by: Charlie Doern <cdoern@redhat.com>

comments.body: not sure what to do with `reauth` or `dockercfg_path` @jwhonce
dockercfg_path would be on the server side or require the client to handle it.  I do not know what reauth is?

@jwhonce it seems that the API does not like the `api.prepare_body` conversion:  `lookup fake_registry: no such host' not found in 'failed to parse request: json: cannot unmarshal string into Go value of type types.AuthConfig'`
@jwhonce @lsm5 @rhatdan PTAL, I'd like to get this in before I cut a release tmrw

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *cdoern*, *lsm5*, *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman-py). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman-py/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [cdoern,lsm5,rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
I think @jwhonce 's change request is blocking

> @jwhonce it seems that the API does not like the `api.prepare_body` conversion: `lookup fake_registry: no such host' not found in 'failed to parse request: json: cannot unmarshal string into Go value of type types.AuthConfig'`

Sounds like the handler is not unmarshaling the data correctly.

> > @jwhonce it seems that the API does not like the `api.prepare_body` conversion: `lookup fake_registry: no such host' not found in 'failed to parse request: json: cannot unmarshal string into Go value of type types.AuthConfig'`
> 
> Sounds like the handler is not unmarshaling the data correctly.

fixed now, I was unnecessarily using json.Dumps
